evals.name: opportunity-capture
evals.prompt: 有个客户反馈说导出功能太慢，经常超时，希望能优化
evals.expected_output: Captures the feedback as a business opportunity. Creates OPP entry with inferred source type (customer-feedback), writes to opportunities.md, and suggests next steps (create Epic or link to existing Epic).

assertions.text: Creates an OPP entry with unique ID (e.g. OPP-XXX)
Infers source type as customer-feedback based on '客户反馈' keyword
Writes opportunity record to opportunities.md
OPP entry contains description capturing the core pain point
OPP entry contains creation date
Suggests next step: create Epic from this opportunity or link to existing Epic
Output uses natural language, no raw internal IDs in user-facing text

evals.name: epic-from-opportunity
evals.expected_output: Reads OPP-001 details, guides user through MoS (Measures of Success) definition, creates EPIC file linked to OPP-001, updates business hierarchy tree.

assertions.text: Reads OPP-001 from opportunities.md to understand context
Guides user to define Measures of Success (MoS) for the Epic
Creates EPIC file with unique ID (e.g. EPIC-XXX)
EPIC file contains link/reference back to OPP-001
EPIC file contains OBJ association if applicable
Updates business hierarchy tree with new Epic entry
Suggests next step: decompose Epic into BRs

evals.name: epic-direct
evals.prompt: 我想创建一个专题来管理用户认证和授权
evals.expected_output: Creates Epic directly without requiring an existing Opportunity. Associates with relevant OBJ if identifiable, creates EPIC file, updates hierarchy.

assertions.text: Creates EPIC file without requiring a pre-existing OPP
Attempts to associate Epic with relevant OBJ from vision/strategy
EPIC file contains description of scope (authentication and authorization)
EPIC file contains MoS section (may prompt user to define)
Updates business hierarchy tree with new Epic
Suggests next step: decompose into BRs or capture related opportunities

evals.name: decompose-epic-to-br
evals.expected_output: Reads EPIC-001 details, suggests BR decomposition plan, waits for user confirmation, creates BR entries linked to the Epic.

assertions.text: Reads EPIC-001 to understand scope and MoS
Proposes BR decomposition plan with rationale
Waits for user confirmation before creating BRs
Creates BR entries with unique IDs linked to EPIC-001
Each BR has clear scope description and acceptance hints
Updates business hierarchy tree showing EPIC->BR relationships
Suggests next step: further decompose BRs into PFs

evals.name: decompose-br-to-pf
evals.expected_output: Reads BR-001 details, suggests PF (Product Feature) decomposition, waits for user confirmation, creates PF entries that bridge business and development.

assertions.text: Reads BR-001 to understand business requirement scope
Proposes PF decomposition plan mapping business needs to product features
Waits for user confirmation before creating PFs
Creates PF entries with unique IDs linked to BR-001
Each PF contains acceptance criteria derived from BR
Updates hierarchy tree showing BR->PF relationships
Suggests next step: use /pace-dev or /pace-plan for implementation planning
PF entries are compatible with existing devpace CR workflow

evals.name: align-check
evals.expected_output: Performs read-only strategic alignment analysis. Reports OBJ coverage rate, identifies orphan entities (Epics without OBJ, BRs without Epic), and highlights misalignment risks.

assertions.text: Performs read-only analysis without modifying any files
Reports OBJ coverage rate (which OBJs have Epics/BRs mapped)
Identifies orphan Epics not linked to any OBJ
Identifies orphan BRs not linked to any Epic
Reports isolated entities (OPPs not converted to Epics)
Provides actionable recommendations for alignment gaps
No files are created or modified during analysis

evals.name: view-panorama
evals.expected_output: Displays read-only business panorama showing full hierarchy: OPP->EPIC->BR->PF->CR with status indicators at each level.

assertions.text: Displays OPP->EPIC->BR->PF->CR hierarchy in structured format
Each entity shows current status indicator
Hierarchy is visually clear with indentation or tree structure
Includes summary statistics (total counts per entity type)
Read-only operation, no files modified
Output uses natural language descriptions alongside entity IDs

evals.name: backward-compat
evals.expected_output: When no Epic/Opportunity layer exists, gracefully falls back to showing existing PF->CR hierarchy. Does not error or require Epic/OPP setup.

assertions.text: Detects absence of Epic/Opportunity layer without error
Falls back to displaying existing PF->CR hierarchy
Does not require or force Epic/OPP creation
Suggests /pace-biz opportunity or /pace-biz epic to enrich business context
Existing devpace workflow remains fully functional
No files are created or modified

evals.name: discover-interactive
evals.prompt: /pace-biz discover 我想做一个任务管理工具
evals.expected_output: Launches multi-turn interactive discovery. Asks goal framing questions, then brainstorms features, defines boundaries, and presents structured candidate tree for confirmation.

assertions.text: Asks goal-framing questions (problem, target users) in first round
Progressively deepens with feature brainstorming questions
Asks boundary definition questions (what is out of scope)
Presents structured candidate tree (OPP->Epic->BR->PF) for confirmation
Persists session state to scope-discovery.md during multi-turn conversation
After confirmation, writes OPP/Epic/BR/PF to .devpace/ files
Cleans up scope-discovery.md after successful completion
Suggests next steps: decompose or /pace-plan next

evals.name: discover-degraded
evals.prompt: /pace-biz discover 做一个聊天机器人
evals.expected_output: Without .devpace/, runs discovery conversation normally but outputs to console instead of writing files. Guides user to /pace-init.

assertions.text: Runs discovery conversation normally without .devpace/
Outputs candidate tree to console, does not write files
Guides user to run /pace-init first
No files are created or modified

evals.name: import-documents
evals.expected_output: Reads the document, auto-detects source type, extracts requirement entities, performs merge analysis against existing feature tree, and presents diff-style plan for confirmation.

assertions.text: Reads the specified file and detects source type
Extracts requirement entities (BR/PF candidates) from document
Performs merge analysis: classifies as NEW/DUPLICATE/ENRICHMENT/CONFLICT
Presents diff-style merge plan for user confirmation
After confirmation, writes new entities to project.md feature tree
Marks imported content with source attribution comment
Does not create CRs (import operates at BR/PF level only)
Suggests /pace-biz align after import

evals.name: infer-codebase
evals.expected_output: Scans codebase structure, mines signals (TODO/FIXME, routes, models), compares against feature tree, and presents three-part gap report.

assertions.text: Scans source code directories for modules, routes, models, components
Mines TODO/FIXME/HACK comments as technical debt signals
Compares inferred features against existing feature tree
Presents three-part report: untracked, unimplemented, technical debt
Allows user to select which items to add to feature tree
Technical debt PFs are suffixed with '(technical debt)' marker
Marks inferred content with source attribution comment
Suggests /pace-biz align after inference

evals.name: infer-no-git
evals.expected_output: Without git, degrades gracefully: skips blame/hotspot analysis, still performs directory structure and comment scanning.

assertions.text: Detects git is unavailable and degrades gracefully
Skips git-enhanced analysis (hotspot, co-change, contributor)
Still performs directory structure analysis and comment scanning
Produces gap report without git-specific sections
Does not error or abort due to missing git

evals.name: refine-br
evals.expected_output: Reads BR-001, displays current readiness score with dimension checklist, asks targeted refinement questions for missing dimensions, writes updates after confirmation, shows readiness score change.

assertions.text: Reads BR-001 from project.md or requirements/BR-001.md
Displays current readiness score with per-dimension status
Asks refinement questions only for missing/insufficient dimensions
Shows update preview before writing (diff format with +/~ markers)
Waits for user confirmation before writing changes
Updates BR content in project.md or overflow file
Shows readiness score change (old% -> new%)
Provides dynamic next-step recommendation based on new readiness level

evals.name: refine-pf
evals.prompt: 细化一下 PF-003 的边界条件
evals.expected_output: Reads PF-003, focuses refinement on boundary conditions as user specified, supplements with other missing dimensions, writes updates after confirmation.

assertions.text: Reads PF-003 from project.md feature tree
Prioritizes boundary conditions questions as user specified
Asks 2-3 questions per round, avoids information overload
Marks updated content with source attribution <!-- source: claude, refined -->
Shows readiness score change after refinement
Skips dimensions user says not sure or later without forcing

evals.name: refine-lite-br-blocked
evals.expected_output: In lite mode, BR refinement is not available. Terminates with guidance to specify PF number or upgrade to full mode.

assertions.text: Detects lite mode from project.md config
Terminates BR refinement with clear explanation
Suggests specifying PF number instead
Mentions /pace-init --upgrade-mode as upgrade path
No files are created or modified

evals.name: discover-lite-mode
evals.prompt: /pace-biz discover 做一个文件管理功能
evals.expected_output: In lite mode, discover skips OPP/Epic/BR layers. Produces OBJ->PF candidate tree directly. Writes PFs to project.md under corresponding OBJ.

assertions.text: Detects lite mode from project.md config
Skips OPP/Epic/BR creation in discovery process
Presents candidate tree as OBJ->PF structure without OPP/Epic/BR
Writes PFs directly under OBJ in project.md
Does not create Epic or BR files

evals.name: empty-arg-guidance
evals.expected_output: With no arguments, scans project context and provides personalized recommendation based on current project lifecycle stage.

assertions.text: Scans opportunities.md, epics/, and project.md for current state
Provides at least one specific recommendation with command
Recommendation is contextually appropriate to project state
Lists available subcommands for reference
No files are created or modified (read-only guidance)
